description_long: This object is a detailed model of the Eiffel Tower, a world-famous iron lattice tower located in Paris, France. The structure features four wide, angled legs that converge as they rise, forming a tall, tapering spire with a small observation platform near the top. The base is set on a square platform that resembles a section of a city map or park, adding context to its urban placement. The tower is characterized by its intricate latticework, visible cross-bracing, and a pointed pinnacle at the summit. The coloration is primarily dark brown or black with lighter highlights, mimicking the real tower's painted iron. No windows or doors are clearly visible, and the model is devoid of antennas or modern communication equipment.
description: A detailed, dark-colored lattice tower modeled after the Eiffel Tower, set on a square base resembling a city park.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide base, four angled legs, and the full height of the tower with its pointed top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the symmetry of the legs and the lattice structure, with the base platform visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the converging legs and the central spire, with the base's map details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the tower's symmetry and the detailed latticework.
